content.problemStatement: In this task, you are required to create a program that declares both an integer and a float for temperature readings.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two numbers separated by a space: an integer temperature value and a floating‑point temperature value (with at most one digit after the decimal point).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output two lines. The first line prints the integer temperature exactly as read. The second line prints the floating‑point temperature with one digit after the decimal point. Do not print extra spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The integer temperature 25 is printed on the first line and the float temperature 77.0 (formatted with one decimal) on the second line.
Two test cases are processed sequentially. Each integer is followed by its corresponding float on the next line.
Demonstrates positive, negative, and zero values. The output respects the required line order and formatting.

solution.approach: The task is purely I/O. Read the number of test cases, then for each case read the integer and the float. Print them in the required order, ensuring the float is formatted with exactly one digit after the decimal point. No computation is needed beyond formatting.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        i = int(next(it))
        f = float(next(it))
        out.append(str(i))
        out.append(f"{f:.1f}")
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
